*Description:*
Holy Cross Abbey (Mainistir na Croise Naofa) was a Cistercian monastery in Holycross near Thurles, County Tipperary, Ireland, situated on the River Suir. It takes its name from a relic of the True Cross or Holy Rood.

*Name:* Wray Castle
*Country:* england
*Region:* northern england
*County:* Cumbria
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 19th century
*Coords:* 54.4006345, -2.9641913
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wray_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Wray%20Castle/@54.381,-2.953,15z
*Main Image:* https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/24/Wray_Castle_2008.jpg/330px-Wray_Castle_2008.jpg
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* unknown
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown

*Description:*
Wray Castle is a Victorian neo-gothic building at Claife in Cumbria within the boundaries of the historic county of Lancashire. The house and grounds have belonged to the National Trust since 1929.
The Castle was open to the public for a dozen years prior to 2024. The Castle is now closed for refurbishment until 2027.

_Listing 6 of 79_

items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Binchester Roman Fort
*Country:* england
*Region:* northern england
*County:* County Durham
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 1st-4th century AD
*Coords:* 54.676, -1.677
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vinovia
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Binchester%20Roman%20Fort/@54.67,-1.6833,15z
*Main Image:* https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/place-photos/AJRVUZPeg-w-eGvncgoFujRQ6sO90YyDoPb5TMF_U7Z7E89jhjsdNtybei6smA0ShWQg45gWsB6qGE7lJpEMYWlqdUtEZkL8yh2ACKwx8ZXVHgnjuA8JEjc2dCYmEhorP24_mlNmAVcglOffvIfAGUg=s4800-w800
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* google
*Has Wikipedia:* unknown
*Has Google Places:* unknown

*Description:*
Vinovia or Vinovium was a Roman fort and settlement situated just over 1 mile (1.6 km) to the north of the town of Bishop Auckland on the banks of the River Wear in County Durham, England. The fort was the site of a hamlet until the late Middle Ages, but the modern-day village of Binchester is about 2 miles (3 km) to the east, near Spennymoor. The ruins are now known as the Binchester Roman Fort.

_Listing 8 of 79_

The fort was the site of a hamlet until the late Middle Ages, but the modern-day village of Binchester is about 2 miles (3 km) to the east, near Spennymoor. The ruins are now known as the Binchester Roman Fort.

items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Croxteth Hall
*Country:* england
*Region:* northern england
*County:* Merseyside
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 16th-20th century
*Coords:* 53.442, -2.891
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Croxteth_Hall
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Croxteth%20Hall/@53.4433,-2.8833,15z
*Main Image:* https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/place-photos/AJRVUZMz-bwrM9Id4c05-4h4QyzOkeX5-kpI90tOPV86XyVPEKIW5ck9Wapl2JbGBcpsWno1rwXUvcS_si-QPhGFF6z3Ji40fR9oZ5Pd2DPWJd_YX8stjvjwDiWDMEYh5JDLPiF6HYh8j_LLrFCqBw=s4800-w1200
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* google
*Has Wikipedia:* unknown
*Has Google Places:* unknown

*Description:*
Croxteth Hall is a country estate and Grade II* listed building in the West Derby suburb of Liverpool, England. It is the former country estate and ancestral home of the Molyneux family, the Earls of Sefton. After the death of the seventh and last Earl in 1972, the estate passed to Liverpool City Council, which now manages the remainder of the estate following the sale of approximately half of the grounds. The remaining grounds, Croxteth Park, were at one time a hunting chase of the Molyneux family and are now open to the public.

_Listing 9 of 79_

listing.name: Croxteth Hall
listing.desc: Croxteth Hall is a country estate and Grade II* listed building in the West Derby suburb of Liverpool, England. It is the former country estate and ancestral home of the Molyneux family, the Earls of Sefton. After the death of the seventh and last Earl in 1972, the estate passed to Liverpool City Council, which now manages the remainder of the estate following the sale of approximately half of the grounds. The remaining grounds, Croxteth Park, were at one time a hunting chase of the Molyneux family and are now open to the public.

items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Oxford Castle
*Country:* england
*Region:* south east london
*County:* Oxfordshire
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 11th century
*Coords:* 51.7517, -1.2632
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oxford_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Oxford%20Castle/@51.7508,-1.2656,15z
*Main Image:* https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/place-photos/AJRVUZM9Pb51ZnHGxwwDFNpxAgUmcVaMg5eGFxsnIFh2Aap0_TNr-CieZMxW4cLAkHX6CtmxZo87JGsVxP32yDf7-7BwNWZMPhlM6M_8EyFNCTpLQK-JGQCwUurlwidS5lwHtVAvLZqFV8P34f0rYNA=s4800-w1200
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* google
*Has Wikipedia:* unknown
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* update google places link - https://maps.app.goo.gl/XYgbWp9ZTRBfpvZX9
add source - https://www.oxfordcastleandprison.co.uk/

*Description:*
Oxford Castle is a large, partly ruined medieval castle on the western side of central Oxford in Oxfordshire, England. Most of the original moated, wooden motte and bailey castle was replaced in stone in the late 12th or early 13th century and the castle played an important role in the conflict of the Anarchy. In the 14th century the military value of the castle diminished and the site became used primarily for county administration and as a prison. The surviving rectangular St George's Tower is now believed to pre-date the remainder of the castle and be a watch tower associated with the original Saxon west gate of the city.

_Listing 10 of 79_

listing.name: Oxford Castle
listing.desc: Oxford Castle is a large, partly ruined medieval castle on the western side of central Oxford in Oxfordshire, England. Most of the original moated, wooden motte and bailey castle was replaced in stone in the late 12th or early 13th century and the castle played an important role in the conflict of the Anarchy. In the 14th century the military value of the castle diminished and the site became used primarily for county administration and as a prison. The surviving rectangular St George's Tower is now believed to pre-date the remainder of the castle and be a watch tower associated with the original Saxon west gate of the city.

imageAttribution.description: View up Castle Mill Stream to St George's Tower of Oxford Castle, England

items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Compton Castle
*Country:* england
*Region:* south west
*County:* Devon
*Type:* fortified house
*Period:* 14th-16th century
*Coords:* 50.4725, -3.6003
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Compton_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Compton%20Castle/@50.4667,-3.5833,15z
*Main Image:* https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/place-photos/AJRVUZN5M_ayQqcxy4A1vn_RWUxdUzvyqg0ZsrrwxAirkE3bg9pl1kUWCRGkF-5JE4JgnfKdj0Gu_XUTrLVKD8C0G9eU5aXhNrC1WmXJUL0KSXYLwgjGYyXGt4eNIt2QtTO7MQxwg-lG4RVghdOu3gI=s4800-w1200
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* google
*Has Wikipedia:* unknown
*Has Google Places:* unknown

*Description:*
Compton Castle in the parish of Marldon in Devon, is a fortified manor house in the village of Compton, about 5 miles (8 km) west of Torquay on the southern coast of Devon, England. The estate was home to the families of Compton, de la Pole, Doddiscombe, Gilbert and Templer. The castle has been home to the Gilbert family for most of the time since it was built. Listed as a Grade 1 set of buildings, it has been a National Trust property since 1951.

_Listing 11 of 79_

listing.name: Compton Castle
listing.desc: Compton Castle in the parish of Marldon in Devon, is a fortified manor house in the village of Compton, about 5 miles (8 km) west of Torquay on the southern coast of Devon, England. The estate was home to the families of Compton, de la Pole, Doddiscombe, Gilbert and Templer. The castle has been home to the Gilbert family for most of the time since it was built. Listed as a Grade 1 set of buildings, it has been a National Trust property since 1951.

*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thornton_Abbey

*Image Source:* wikipedia
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* update google places link - https://maps.app.goo.gl/HU2rsfHVVRTBs53cA

*Description:*
Thornton Abbey was a medieval abbey located close to the small North Lincolnshire village of Thornton Curtis, near Ulceby, and directly south of Hull on the other side of the Humber estuary. Its ruins are a Grade I listed building, including notably England's largest and most impressive surviving monastic gatehouse.

_Listing 12 of 79_

*Country:* england
*Region:* the midlands

*Type:* castle
*Period:* 18th century

*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stainsby_Mill
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Stainsby%20Mill/@53.1583,-1.3183,15z
*Main Image:* https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/place-photos/AJRVUZOX8kXSmx5D38D9vCO7Q4ffdL6qCqhNKcQswrGBulY-hruzo32mGEqUjvsWBlWGCBXWInySKIxTbwd0AX9qUokpQ62RvTUVwQibW7ppSf8D3sgA7AUIZxYvcD1MyeEezGF7aNgUeU-ZfByrQQ=s4800-w1200
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* google
*Has Wikipedia:* unknown
*Has Google Places:* unknown

*Description:*
Stainsby Mill is a 19th-century flour watermill in Doe Lea, Chesterfield, Derbyshire, England. The mill, which has been restored to full working order, is a Grade II listed building and is under the ownership of the National Trust. The mill is part of the Hardwick Hall estate.

_Listing 13 of 79_

Hellens Manor is a historic house located in the village of Much Marcle in Herefordshire, England. It is one of the oldest dwellings in England, being composed primarily of Tudor, Jacobean and Georgian architecture but with foundations dating from the 12th century.

*Country:* wales
*Region:* mid wales marches
*County:* Powys
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 13th century
*Coords:* 52.5462, -3.252
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dolforwyn_Castle

*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* CDN
*Has Wikipedia:* yes

*Description:*
Dolforwyn Castle is a Welsh medieval castle above the village of Abermule, Powys. The fortification was established by Llywelyn ap Gruffudd, Prince of Gwynedd in the late 13th century. It is sited on a wooded ridge commanding excellent views of the upper Severn Valley.

_Listing 16 of 79_
